content.problemStatement:
Chef aims to be the richest person in Chefland by his new restaurant franchise. Currently, his assets are worth AAA billion dollars and have no liabilities. He aims to increase his assets by XXX billion dollars per year.

Also, all the richest people in Chefland are not planning to grow and maintain their current worth.

To be the richest person in Chefland, he needs to be worth at least BBB billion dollars. How many years will it take Chef to reach his goal if his value increases by XXX billion dollars each year?
content.inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case contains a single line of input, three integers AAA, BBB, XXX.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output in a single line the answer to the problem.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 111: Chef needs to increase his worth by 200−100=100200 - 100 = 100200−100=100 billion dollars and his increment per year being 101010 billion dollars, so it will take him 10010=10\frac{100}{10} = 1010100​=10 years to do so.
Test Case 222: Chef needs to increase his worth by 199−111=88199 - 111 = 88199−111=88 billion dollars and his increment per year being 111111 billion dollars, so it will take him 8811=8\frac{88}{11} = 81188​=8 years to do so.
Test Case 333: Chef needs to increase his worth by 200−190=10200 - 190 = 10200−190=10 billion dollars and his increment per year being 101010 billion dollars, so it will take him 1010=1\frac{10}{10} = 11010​=1 year to do so.
